summary: Command substitution eats exports in POSIX sh
detail: Calling _fixture="$(setup_sandbox)" in test-sandbox-guard.sh caused HOME/XDG_CONFIG_HOME/XDG_STATE_HOME/FURROW_ROOT exports to die with the subshell. Pattern: call setup_sandbox >/dev/null (no subshell) and capture the fixture path via tempfile when both the env-var exports and the path are needed.

summary: Cross-deliverable file_ownership boundaries surface deferred ACs mid-wave
detail: script-modes-fix's AC-4 (register hook via install.sh merge into .claude/settings.json) requires a one-line edit to install.sh, which is outside its file_ownership. install.sh is owned by specialist-symlink-unification. Parallel wave-2 dispatch created an orphan AC that neither agent can close alone. Resolution at implement time: specialist-symlink-unification agent adds the hook registration line while touching install.sh for its own AC. Decompose step should have caught this — file_ownership partitioning isolates content but not cross-AC dependencies.

summary: Parallel specialists on same branch need staging hygiene
detail: wave-2 parallel agents share the working tree. One agent (specialist-symlink-unification) ran 'git rm --cached' for 14 files; another (script-modes-fix) had those deletions pre-staged in its index when it went to commit. Pattern: every specialist must run 'git diff --cached --name-only' pre-commit and 'git restore --staged <paths-outside-ownership>' if cross-deliverable staging is present. Consider making this a harness check in a pre-commit hook.

summary: Spec must verify CLI flags against actual implementation
detail: script-modes-fix spec referenced 'bin/frw rescue --diagnose' but rescue.sh has no --diagnose flag — diagnose is the default with --apply as the opt-in write. Agent adapted at implement time. Future spec step should grep the actual CLI surface when writing verification commands.

summary: Shared libs under sandboxed FURROW_ROOT must resolve paths relative to script location
detail: When validate-json.sh is sourced from a script that runs under a sandbox-local FURROW_ROOT, that FURROW_ROOT doesn't contain the lib/ or schemas/ directories. Resolve shared library and schema paths via $(dirname "$0")/../lib/... and $(dirname "$0")/../../../schemas/... — NOT via $FURROW_ROOT. Adopt for any future shared lib sourced from sandboxed scripts.

summary: setup_sandbox reuses $TMP across test functions in same script
detail: setup_sandbox honors existing $TMP if already exported. Across test functions in one script, this causes git init conflicts. Convention: 'unset TMP' at the top of each test function. Future work: add a reset_sandbox helper to lib/sandbox.sh that explicitly unsets state between tests.

summary: Required evidence_path forces sentinel for no-review case
detail: Generator previously emitted {pass: false} with no evidence_path when no review existed. New schema-required field forced a sentinel reviews/none.md. Consumers (notably /furrow:merge) must treat reviews/none.md as 'no review evidence' rather than attempting to dereference a missing file.

summary: Parallel specialists on a shared worktree cause commit message/content swaps via git-index races
detail: During wave-2 parallel dispatch, ac-10 and xdg-config agents interleaved git-add and git-commit calls. Result: commit 5232e94 has ac-10's commit message but xdg-config's files; commit 9463108 has correct ac-10 content. Both agents' content landed cleanly, but attribution is cosmetically wrong in one commit. Root cause: no index-lock across parallel bash calls on the same branch. Mitigations: (a) use git worktrees per deliverable, (b) add a mutex around commit operations in parallel harness, (c) each agent creates a deliverable-specific branch and the orchestrator merges sequentially.

summary: Auto-install hooks rewrite symlinks during parallel agent runs
detail: install.sh re-ran several times during wave-2 (triggered by agent tool calls), each time normalizing .claude/commands/furrow:*.md symlinks from '../../../furrow/commands/X.md' (external) to '../../commands/X.md' (self-hosted). The normalization is correct policy but the changes showed up as uncommitted working-tree edits mid-wave, causing cross-deliverable staging confusion. Future: gate auto-install execution behind a 'is orchestrator session' check, or make its changes idempotent (already-normalized targets are no-ops).

summary: stop-ideation.sh now sources common.sh, potentially breaking test-hook-cascade invariant
detail: xdg-config-consumer-wiring changed stop-ideation.sh to source common.sh (for resolve_config_value) instead of common-minimal.sh. test-hook-cascade.sh asserts 'no hooks reference lib/common.sh' — this invariant will now fail. Options: (a) move resolve_config_value to common-minimal.sh (rejected: needs yq, not hook-safe), (b) whitelist stop-ideation.sh in the invariant check, (c) split the invariant. Out-of-scope for this row; follow-up TODO needed.
